results.description: STEP-01: the full D1-D5 plan (ideal ≥ 5 half-day activities)
STEP-01: D1 logistics checklist (access, tools, meetings)
STEP-01: questions that must be asked on D1

kickoffPlan.activity: Kickoff meeting with Sponsor (COO) + CIO: validate project charter, confirm mandate, business objectives, success criteria, in/out-of-scope boundaries and governance/escalation path.
Onboarding & logistics: badges/physical access, laptop/VPN/SSO, collaboration tools (Confluence/Jira/MS Project/Teams); team introductions; initial stakeholder mapping and power/interest grid.
One-on-one deep dive with CIO: IS landscape, legacy constraints, data ownership, and the contractual/scope perimeter of the two incumbent SSII.
Delivery team working session: roles & capacity, ways of working (hybrid Agile/Waterfall), tooling, and establish the RAID log baseline.
CHRO meeting: post-reorg context and mood, change-readiness assessment, HR/social sensitivities, and co-design of the internal communication plan (PROSCI ADKAR).
Legacy IS discovery workshop with IT: architecture walk-through, integration points, data availability/quality assessment on the undocumented estate.
Business unit interviews: elicit pain points and candidate AI use cases; capture value hypotheses and feasibility signals.
Alignment meeting with the two incumbent SSII: clarify scope boundaries, collaboration model, and information-sharing to prevent overlap and defuse tension.
Synthesis & prioritization: consolidate findings, WSJF scoring of candidate use cases, draft the D5 scoping note and the D1-D30 engagement plan.
Steering readout with COO + CIO: present RAG status and scoping note, validate prioritized use cases and D1-D30 plan, confirm next-step decisions and owners.

logisticsChecklist: Physical site access and badges arranged for the two on-site days per week
Laptop, VPN, SSO/network accounts and email/distribution-list provisioning confirmed
Access granted to collaboration stack (Confluence, Jira, MS Project, Teams/SharePoint) and shared deliverables workspace
Read access to IS documentation, architecture diagrams and available data catalogs (NDA / data-room clearance confirmed)
Meeting rooms booked and calendar invites sent to COO, CIO, CHRO, delivery team, SSII contacts for D1-D5
On-site cadence (2 days/week) and remote-working tooling validated with the client
Stakeholder contact directory and escalation contacts collected
Introduction email issued and D1 report template pre-populated and ready
d1Questions: What are the top 3 business outcomes the COO expects from this 3-month AI scoping, and how will success be measured?
What is explicitly out of scope, and what is the governance / decision-making and escalation path?
What is the contractual perimeter of the two incumbent SSII and where are the scope boundaries with them?
What legacy IS documentation exists, who owns it, and who are the key data gatekeepers?
What budget envelope and regulatory constraints apply (EU AI Act, GDPR)?
Given the recent reorganization, what communication to the teams is authorized, and what are the known resistance points?
Which AI use cases or initiatives are already on the table or expected?
Who must sign off on the D5 scoping note and the D1-D30 plan?
kickoffRisks: Political: post-reorg tension may reduce stakeholder availability and candor, slowing elicitation (HR-sensitive).
Political: the two incumbent SSII may perceive the engagement as a threat and withhold information or resist collaboration.
Technical: undocumented legacy IS makes discovery slower than planned, with hidden dependencies and data-access delays.
HR: CHRO sensitivity on the 'AI vs jobs' narrative post-reorg requires carefully framed change communication.
Governance: ambiguity between Sponsor (COO) mandate and CIO authority could create decision bottlenecks.
Scope: broad 'AI scoping' ambition within 3 months creates scope-creep risk without early WSJF prioritization.
Data: unknown data availability/quality, with access gated through incumbent SSII, may delay feasibility assessment.

results.description: STEP-02: simplified IS mapping (systems in place, non-empty)
STEP-02: grey areas to clarify on D1

output.clientContext:
CLIENT CONTEXT SHEET (v0.1 — to be confirmed at D1 kickoff; sector not yet disclosed in the brief and flagged as a grey area).

• Sector / activity: Mid-to-large enterprise engaging a 3-month AI scoping mission. Exact sector to be confirmed on D1; the presence of a COO, CIO, CHRO, two incumbent SSII and a regulated-data posture (EU AI Act + GDPR) points to a structured, mid/large services or industrial organization.

• Strategic stakes: (1) Identify and prioritize high-value, feasible AI/GenAI use cases within 3 months; (2) build a credible D1–D30 engagement plan and a D5 scoping note validated by COO + CIO; (3) de-risk delivery on an undocumented legacy IS with data access gated through incumbent SSII; (4) secure regulatory compliance (EU AI Act risk tiering, GDPR lawful basis, data minimization).

• Sponsorship & mandate: Sponsor = COO (Accountable); direct reporting line = CIO (Responsible / line manager). Dual-authority configuration (business sponsor vs. IT authority) to be arbitrated early to avoid decision bottlenecks.

• Culture: Post-reorganization context — sensitive social climate, reduced stakeholder availability/candor, and an "AI vs. jobs" narrative to be handled carefully with the CHRO. Change readiness to be assessed via PROSCI ADKAR; communication plan co-designed with HR. Hybrid delivery culture (Agile/Waterfall), 2 days/week on-site.

• Competitive / ecosystem: Two incumbent SSII (integrators) hold contractual scope and act as data gatekeepers on the legacy estate; they may perceive the AI mission as a threat — collaboration model and information-sharing to be clarified early to defuse tension and prevent overlap. External competitive landscape (market rivals) to be captured during D4 business-unit interviews.

isMapping.owner: IT / IS architects (to be named on D2)
isMapping.aiRelevance: Primary source of business data for candidate use cases
Feature/data availability drives AI feasibility scoring (WSJF)
In-scope only with explicit HR + legal clearance

isMapping.system: Interfaces / APIs / batch flows managed by incumbent SSII
isMapping.documentation: Held by SSII — access to be negotiated
isMapping.aiRelevance: Access path for data feasibility; potential collaboration friction
Deliverable and governance backbone for the mission

isMapping.system: IAM / SSO / VPN, data-room / NDA controls
isMapping.aiRelevance: EU AI Act + GDPR guardrails for any AI use case

output.orgChart:
ORGANIZATIONAL CHART (business + IT) — as inferred from the kickoff brief; names/details to be completed on D1.

BUSINESS SIDE
• COO — Project Sponsor (Accountable). Owns the mandate, business objectives and success criteria; final sign-off authority on the D5 scoping note.
   └─ Business Units / Process Owners (Consulted) — source of pain points and candidate AI use cases (D4-AM interviews).
   └─ End users & impacted employees (Informed) — targets of change management (post-reorg sensitivity).
• CHRO — (Consulted). Owns post-reorg change-readiness, HR/social sensitivities and co-design of the internal communication plan (PROSCI ADKAR); gatekeeper on HR data.

IT SIDE
• CIO — Direct manager / line authority (Responsible). Owns IS landscape, legacy constraints, data ownership and the contractual relationship with the two incumbent SSII.
   └─ IT / IS Architects & Data Owners (Consulted) — legacy architecture, integration points, data availability/quality.
   └─ Incumbent SSII #1 & #2 (Consulted / external) — contractual scope holders and data gatekeepers on the legacy estate.

MISSION TEAM (cross-cutting)
• AI Project Manager / Engagement Lead (Responsible) — this role; drives scoping, elicitation, governance and reporting.
   └─ Delivery team (Responsible) — capacity, ways of working (hybrid Agile/Waterfall), RAID log baseline.

GOVERNANCE / ESCALATION: COO (Sponsor, arbitration) ← CIO (line + IT decisions) ← AI PM (operational steering). Dual-authority interface (COO vs. CIO) to be clarified on D1 to prevent decision bottlenecks.

glossary.definition: Maîtrise d'ouvrage / Assistance à MOA — the business-ownership side steering needs, specs, UAT and change on behalf of the sponsor.
IT services & integration company; here the two incumbent integrators holding contractual scope and acting as data gatekeepers.
Chief Information Officer — direct manager and IT authority (Responsible).
Chief Human Resources Officer — owns change-readiness and HR/social sensitivities (Consulted).
Weighted Shortest Job First — prioritization score (value / effort proxy) used to rank candidate AI use cases at D5.
Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies register — mission control baseline set at D2.
Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed — stakeholder responsibility matrix.
Red/Amber/Green health indicator used in steering readouts.
Awareness-Desire-Knowledge-Ability-Reinforcement — change-management model framing internal AI communication.
European regulation tiering AI systems by risk; drives compliance guardrails for use cases.
General Data Protection Regulation — governs personal-data processing (lawful basis, minimization), critical for HR data.
First-30-days engagement plan delivered and validated at D5.
Consolidated findings + prioritized use cases; requires COO + CIO sign-off.
Decision gate on prioritized use cases and next-step ownership.
Generative AI / Large Language Models — candidate technology family for content and process use cases.

greyAreas: Sector / business activity of the client is not stated in the brief — confirm on D1 to tailor use-case value hypotheses and competitive analysis.
Top 3 measurable business outcomes expected by the COO and the exact success metrics for the 3-month scoping.
Explicit out-of-scope boundaries and the governance/decision/escalation path — especially the COO (Sponsor) vs. CIO (line authority) arbitration to avoid decision bottlenecks.
Contractual perimeter of the two incumbent SSII and the precise scope boundary with the mission (data access, deliverable ownership, collaboration model).
Existence, ownership and location of legacy IS documentation and data catalogs; identity of the key data gatekeepers.
Budget envelope and applicable regulatory constraints (EU AI Act risk tier, GDPR lawful basis for any personal/HR data).
Authorized level of internal communication to teams post-reorg and the known/anticipated resistance points (CHRO-framed).
AI use cases or initiatives already on the table or expected by leadership, to avoid re-litigating settled decisions.
Formal sign-off authority on the D5 scoping note and the D1–D30 plan (single vs. joint COO+CIO approval).
Data availability and quality on the undocumented estate — the main feasibility blocker gating WSJF scoring.

results.description: STEP-03: map of allies, neutrals, and potential resisters (non-empty)
STEP-03: D1-D30 engagement plan (who to see, when, why — non-empty)

engagementPlan.objective: Establish sponsorship clarity and dispel the AI-vs-jobs narrative before it hardens
actions: Confirm sector, top-3 measurable COO outcomes and success metrics (grey area #1-2)
Arbitrate the COO (Sponsor) vs CIO (line) decision path in a single joint session to prevent bottlenecks
Adopt a listening/observer posture — capture social climate and candor level post-reorg
Agree with CHRO the authorized level of internal communication to teams
engagementPlan.kpi: Signed mandate + agreed escalation path; sponsor coalition confirmed

engagementPlan.objective: Map impacts and de-risk the SSII relationship early
actions: Build RAID log and impact map of affected populations
Run a PROSCI ADKAR change-readiness assessment with HR
Hold an early clarification session with the two incumbent SSII on scope boundary, data-access model and collaboration protocol to defuse the perceived threat
Name IS architects and data gatekeepers
engagementPlan.kpi: ADKAR baseline scored; SSII collaboration charter drafted; RAID baselined

engagementPlan.objective: Build desire through participation and a credible, HR-co-designed narrative
actions: Business-unit interviews to source pain points, candidate use cases and competitive landscape
Co-design change communication plan with CHRO (key messages, channels, cadence, AI-augmentation-not-replacement framing)
Recruit change ambassadors within business units to relay messages peer-to-peer
Address end-user and SSII resistance with targeted, evidence-based reassurance
engagementPlan.kpi: Comms plan validated by CHRO; ambassador network stood up; interview coverage %

engagementPlan.objective: Ground use-case prioritization in data reality and compliance
actions: Assess data availability/quality on the legacy estate (main feasibility blocker) via SSII-gated access
Apply EU AI Act risk tiering and GDPR lawful-basis / minimization checks, especially on HRIS
Feed feasibility + compliance signals into WSJF scoring of candidate use cases
engagementPlan.owners: AI PM + Data Owners + IT Security + Legal
engagementPlan.kpi: WSJF-scored shortlist; each use case tagged with AI-Act tier & GDPR status

engagementPlan.objective: Secure joint sign-off and embed adoption measurement
actions: Consolidate D5 scoping note with prioritized use cases and D1-D30 plan
Obtain COO + CIO joint sign-off (clarify single vs joint authority)
Define adoption KPIs (usage rate, satisfaction, skills uplift) and reinforcement mechanisms for the next phase
Communicate outcomes and next-step ownership to teams within the CHRO-authorized boundary
engagementPlan.kpi: Scoping note signed off; adoption KPI baseline & reinforcement plan agreed

output.d1Posture: observer — in a post-reorganization, socially sensitive context with reduced candor, lead D1 by listening and diagnosing (assess ADKAR readiness, surface the AI-vs-jobs narrative, clarify the COO/CIO dual authority) before shifting to an expert/actor posture from D2 onward.
quickWins: Convene a single early joint COO+CIO session to settle the dual-authority decision path — removes the biggest bottleneck and signals unified sponsorship.
Hold an early, respectful clarification meeting with the two incumbent SSII framing them as partners, not threatened parties — converts likely resisters and unlocks data access.
Co-brand the first internal message with the CHRO around 'AI augments, does not replace' to pre-empt the AI-vs-jobs resistance among impacted employees.
Stand up a small change-ambassador network from business units interviewed at D4 — turns consulted stakeholders into desire-building allies.
Publish an early, honest RAID + grey-areas register to the sponsors, demonstrating candor and control and building trust quickly.

results.description: STEP-05: D1 kit (kickoff plan + client sheet + key questions) present
STEP-05: D5 scoping note (first-days review + adjustments) present

output.d1Kit:
# D1 Kit — AI Mission Kickoff & Alignment

> **Mission:** 30-day AI opportunity scoping & change-readiness diagnostic
> **Context:** Post-reorganization, socially sensitive, reduced candor
> **D1 posture:** Observer — listen and diagnose before shifting to expert/actor from D2
> **Golden thread:** *AI augments, it does not replace.*

---

## 1. Kickoff Plan (D1 agenda)

| Time | Sequence | Objective | Lead |
|---|---|---|---|
| 09:00–09:20 | **Welcome & framing** | Set the observer posture; restate mandate, scope, 30-day horizon | AI PM |
| 09:20–10:00 | **Sponsor alignment (COO)** | Confirm sector, top-3 measurable outcomes, success metrics | COO + AI PM |
| 10:00–10:45 | **Dual-authority arbitration (COO + CIO)** | Settle single vs joint decision path & escalation to remove the #1 bottleneck | COO + CIO |
| 10:45–11:15 | **Change & comms boundary (CHRO)** | Agree authorized level of internal communication + AI-augmentation framing | CHRO + AI PM |
| 11:15–12:00 | **Working setup** | Access, data owners, IS architects, SSII contacts, meeting cadence | AI PM + CIO |
| 14:00–15:30 | **Listening rounds** | Informal exchanges to read social climate & candor post-reorg | AI PM |
| 15:30–16:00 | **RAID & grey-areas capture** | Log open questions, risks, assumptions transparently | AI PM |
| 16:00–16:30 | **D1 wrap & next-step confirmation** | Recap decisions, owners, D2–D3 diagnostic plan | AI PM + sponsors |

**Success at end of D1:** signed/verbal mandate, agreed escalation path, confirmed sponsor coalition, comms boundary set with CHRO.

---

## 2. Client Sheet (stakeholder cartography)

| Stakeholder | RACI | Stance | Engagement priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| **COO** — Mission Sponsor | Accountable | 🟢 Ally | Protect & leverage; single joint session to lock authority |
| **CIO** — Line authority / IT owner | Responsible | ⚪ Neutral | Convert to co-sponsor; unified message with COO |
| **CHRO** — Change & HR data gatekeeper | Consulted | ⚪ Neutral | Co-design narrative; HRIS = GDPR-sensitive |
| **Business Units / Process Owners** | Consulted | ⚪ Neutral | Interview D4+; source use cases & pain points |
| **IS Architects & Data Owners** | Consulted | ⚪ Neutral | Feasibility & data-quality assessment |
| **End users / impacted employees** | Informed | 🔴 Resister | Reassure via ambassadors; augmentation framing |
| **Incumbent SSII #1 & #2** — data gatekeepers | Consulted (external) | 🔴 Resister | Early clarification meeting; partners, not threats |

**Two critical dynamics to manage from D1:**
1. **COO/CIO dual authority** → decision bottleneck risk.
2. **SSII + end-user resistance** → data-access and adoption risk.

---

## 3. Key Questions (to close the grey areas)

### To the COO (Sponsor)
1. In one sentence, what does *success* look like at D+30 — and at D+180?
2. What are your **top-3 measurable outcomes**, and how are they currently measured?
3. Which sector-specific constraints (regulatory, competitive, seasonal) must frame our use-case selection?
4. Where do you personally see the biggest resistance — and the biggest quick win?

### To the CIO (Line authority)
5. For scoping decisions, is authority **yours, the COO's, or joint**? What is the escalation path?
6. Who are the named IS architects and data owners I should engage this week?
7. What are the hard technical/compliance constraints on the legacy estate?

### To the CHRO (Change gatekeeper)
8. What level of communication to teams is authorized, and through which channels?
9. What is the current social climate and candor level post-reorganization?
10. Which HR data can I access, and under what GDPR lawful basis?

### To the incumbent SSII (data gatekeepers)
11. What is the current scope boundary, data-access model and collaboration protocol?
12. What would make this collaboration a **win for you**?

### Cross-cutting (RAID seeds)
13. What has been tried on AI/data before, and why did it stall?
14. What is off-limits, politically or contractually, that I should know now?
output.introEmail:
**Subject:** Kickoff tomorrow — quick intro & how I'll work in the first days

Dear [Manager first name],

Ahead of our start tomorrow, a short note to introduce myself and set expectations for the first days.

I'm Guy Hui-Bon-Hoa, the AI Product Owner / project manager who will lead the 30-day AI opportunity scoping and change-readiness diagnostic. My aim over these four weeks is simple: identify a short, credible list of high-value, feasible AI use cases and a clear D1–D30 plan — grounded in your data reality, compliance constraints (EU AI Act, GDPR) and your teams' readiness.

**A word on my posture for Day 1.** Given the recent reorganization, I'll begin by *listening and observing* rather than prescribing. My first priority is to understand your outcomes, your constraints and your people before proposing anything. I move to a more active, expert posture from Day 2 onward.

**Three things that would help us start fast tomorrow:**
1. A short joint moment with the COO and CIO to align on the decision path and escalation — this removes the single biggest source of delay.
2. A brief exchange with the CHRO on the level of internal communication we're authorized to share with teams.
3. Contacts for the IS architects, data owners and the two incumbent partners so I can plan the diagnostic phase.

One principle will guide every message I help shape internally: **AI augments people's work — it does not replace them.** I'll co-design that narrative with HR before anything goes to the teams.

I'll come with a light agenda (attached) and adapt it to your priorities. Please tell me if there's anyone I should meet first, or anything sensitive I should be aware of before we begin.

Looking forward to getting started.

Best regards,
**Guy Hui-Bon-Hoa**
AI Product Owner — Mission scoping & change readiness
[phone] · guyhui@proton.me

output.d5ScopingNote:
# D5 Scoping Note — First-Days Review & Adjustments

> **Purpose:** Consolidate what the first five days revealed, confirm what holds, and adjust the D1–D30 trajectory before entering elicitation and feasibility.
> **Audience:** COO (Sponsor), CIO, CHRO · **Register:** executive · **Length target:** 1 page

---

## 1. Executive Summary
In the first five days we [confirmed sponsorship / clarified authority / mapped resistance]. The mission is **[on track / adjusted]**. The single most important adjustment is **[…]**. No blocker prevents entering the elicitation phase (D4–D15) as planned.

## 2. What the First Days Confirmed
- **Sponsorship & outcomes:** COO top-3 outcomes validated: 1)… 2)… 3)…
- **Decision path:** COO/CIO authority now [single / joint]; escalation path agreed.
- **Change readiness (ADKAR baseline):** Awareness [low/med/high] · Desire [low/med/high] — main gap: **[…]**.
- **SSII relationship:** clarification meeting held; collaboration charter [drafted / pending]; data-access model: **[…]**.
- **Comms boundary:** CHRO authorized [level]; "AI augments, not replaces" framing adopted.

## 3. What Changed vs the Initial Plan
| Assumption at D1 | Reality at D5 | Adjustment |
|---|---|---|
| [Authority clear] | [Still ambiguous on X] | [Single joint arbitration session on D6] |
| [SSII cooperative] | [Guarded on data] | [Phased access + charter sign-off before D16] |
| [Candor sufficient] | [Reduced post-reorg] | [Ambassador network + peer relay] |

## 4. Risks & Grey Areas (RAID extract)
| # | Risk / Issue | Impact | Mitigation | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| R1 | Data availability/quality on legacy estate | High — feasibility blocker | SSII-gated access plan | Data Owners |
| R2 | End-user AI-vs-jobs resistance | Medium — adoption | Co-branded CHRO comms + ambassadors | AI PM + CHRO |
| R3 | Dual-authority decision latency | Medium — schedule | Locked escalation path | COO + CIO |

## 5. Use-Case Funnel (early signal, to be WSJF-scored D16–D25)
- Candidate use cases surfaced: [n] — top themes: **[…]**.
- Each to be tagged with **EU AI Act tier** + **GDPR lawful basis** (HRIS = high scrutiny).

## 6. Adjusted Plan to D30
| Phase | Window | Key adjustment |
|---|---|---|
| Elicit & co-design comms | D4–D15 | Add ambassador recruitment earlier |
| Feasibility & guardrails | D16–D25 | Front-load data-quality assessment via SSII |
| Scoping note & Go/No-Go | D26–D30 | Confirm single vs joint sign-off with COO+CIO |

## 7. Decisions Requested from Sponsors
1. Confirm the [single/joint] sign-off authority for the D30 Go/No-Go.
2. Approve the SSII collaboration charter to unlock data access.
3. Endorse the co-branded internal message with HR.

## 8. Next Milestone
**D15 — Communication plan validated by CHRO + ambassador network stood up.**
